headings.text: Six reporters, one beat: the slow collapse of the interior.
Five years on the fracture line.

paragraphs.text: Former Pulitzer-finalist environmental reporter. Founded the Dispatch after twelve years covering EPA enforcement gaps. Reports from Tulsa and the southern plains.
Specializes in oilfield financial disclosures and bonded liability. Holds a master's in forensic accounting. Has broken 11 stories on undercapitalized abandonment in the Permian.
Data journalist with a background in hydrology. Builds the Dispatch's groundwater and emissions models. Previously at a national data-investigations team in Washington.
Based in eastern Montana. Covers displacement, ranching collapse, and tribal water rights across the high plains. Drives roughly 60,000 miles a year for the beat.
Photographer and cartographer. Designs the Dispatch's broadsheet identity and longform layouts. Trained in GIS and architectural drawing at Cranbrook.
Manages reader-source intake, FOIA tracking, and the public document library. Previously coordinated a regional public-records consortium for six midwestern outlets.

We exist to report what the regulators will not — the small, accumulating fractures across the American interior that, taken together, describe a continent being remade by industrial negligence. We refuse euphemism. We name operators, document harm, and stay until the story is finished.
We disclose every dollar over one hundred. If a funder asks for influence, they get a refund and a footnote in the next annual report.
Every factual claim of harm is documented by at least two independent sources, one of which must be a primary record.
Corrections appear at the top of the affected story, with the original text preserved in a visible diff.
Sources are named whenever possible. Anonymity is granted only after a documented risk assessment.
Reporter ties to subjects, funders, or shareholders are disclosed inline on each piece.
We use machine analysis for data only. No published prose is machine-written.
For tips, document submissions, and reprint inquiries. Encrypted intake is available on request.
Independent investigative reporting on environmental collapse, industrial negligence, and climate displacement across the American interior.
A Friday letter from the editor with the week's investigations and field notes.

visibleText: Vol. VI · Independent Press About the Dispatch Fracture Line Dispatch Front Investigation Data Desk Field Notes About Fracture Line Dispatch Reporting the cracks before they widen — since 2019 The Newsroom Six reporters, one beat: the slow collapse of the interior. MR Editor in Chief Maren Rieske Former Pulitzer-finalist environmental reporter. Founded the Dispatch after twelve years covering EPA enforcement gaps. Reports from Tulsa and the southern plains. DC Senior Investigative Devon Castaño Specializes in oilfield financial disclosures and bonded liability. Holds a master's in forensic accounting. Has broken 11 stories on undercapitalized abandonment in the Permian. SO Data Desk Lead Shira Olwen Data journalist with a background in hydrology. Builds the Dispatch's groundwater and emissions models. Previously at a national data-investigations team in Washington. JT Field Correspondent Junius Thal Based in eastern Montana. Covers displacement, ranching collapse, and tribal water rights across the high plains. Drives roughly 60,000 miles a year for the beat. AP Visuals Editor Ayodele Park Photographer and cartographer. Designs the Dispatch's broadsheet identity and longform layouts. Trained in GIS and architectural drawing at Cranbrook. EK Engagement Editor Esme Kovacs Manages reader-source intake, FOIA tracking, and the public document library. Previously coordinated a regional public-records consortium for six midwestern outlets. We exist to report what the regulators will not — the small, accumulating fractures across the American interior that, taken together, describe a continent being remade by industrial negligence. We refuse euphemism. We name operators, document harm, and stay until the story is finished. Transparency How the Dispatch is funded. Funder Amount Year Independence Clause Holcombe Trust for Public Reporting $420,000 2024 Full editorial firewall; no review of stories pre-publication. Midcontinent Civic Foundation $215,000 2024 General operating support; no topical restrictions. Reader Member Donations (4,812) $308,500 2024 Anonymized; donor identities sealed from newsroom. Plains Watershed Reporting Fund $140,000 2023 Restricted to aquifer reporting; no story approval rights. Knight–Brennan Local News Grant $95,000 2023 Restricted to data-desk infrastructure. Estate of Cordelia Anning $60,000 2022 Bequest, unrestricted, no conditions. Founding Reader Circle $48,000 2019 Seed funding; no editorial involvement. We disclose every dollar over one hundred. If a funder asks for influence, they get a refund and a footnote in the next annual report. — Maren Rieske, Editor in Chief Editorial Standards 01 / Sourcing Two independent confirmations Every factual claim of harm is documented by at least two independent sources, one of which must be a primary record. 02 / Corrections Public, dated, permanent Corrections appear at the top of the affected story, with the original text preserved in a visible diff. 03 / Anonymity Only when retaliation is real Sources are named whenever possible. Anonymity is granted only after a documented risk assessment. 04 / Conflicts Disclosed in byline Reporter ties to subjects, funders, or shareholders are disclosed inline on each piece. 05 / AI Policy No generative text in reporting We use machine analysis for data only. No published prose is machine-written. Milestones Five years on the fracture line. 2019 Dispatch founded in Tulsa 2020 First Permian leak series 2021 Data Desk launched 2021 National Reporting Prize 2022 Ogallala investigation 2023 Field bureau in Montana 2023 Public document library 2024 Methane Rising series Press & Contact For tips, document submissions, and reprint inquiries. Encrypted intake is available on request. Editorial tips@fracturelinedispatch.org Mailing Address Fracture Line Dispatch 412 N. Boulder Avenue, Suite 3 Tulsa, Oklahoma 74103 United States Fracture Line Dispatch Independent investigative reporting on environmental collapse, industrial negligence, and climate displacement across the American interior.
